title: Use dotnet self-contained deployment
author: o-l-a-v
body_snippet:
Instead of requiring users to install the correct dotnet runtime, consider using a self-contained deployment.

* <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/#publish-self-contained>

Paint.NET has used this since v4.3 released September 2021:

* <https://forums.getpaint.net/topic/118660-paintnet-43-is-now-available/>

Yes, installer will become bigger. But it's arguably worth it vs. all troubleshooting and scenarios one have to think about.

comments.author: winromulus
comments.snippet: It would be great to have to builds for this. If I have dotnet installed, I'd like a smaller installer.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `891cd44b` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Extend CI/CD release workflow to produce two artifacts: a fr

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Ou...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Valid infrastructure enhancement to provide both self-contained and framework-dependent release artifacts, improving deployment flexibility for users with and without .NET installed.
Approach: Extend CI/CD release workflow to produce two artifacts: a framework-dependent build (smaller, requires .NET) and a self-contained build (bundles runtime); build.ps1 already has -SelfContained flag support so mainly needs CI pipeline changes and installer packaging for both variants.
